english: v. sub taṅ-po (thang po); taṅ-śiṅ (thang shing) v. taṅ (thang) comp.

1. the direction straight forward, steṅ daṅ _og daṅ tád-ka tams-cád-du (steng dang 'og dang thad ka thams cad du) upward and downward, and in every other direction Stg.; steṅ-_og-tád-kar (steng 'og thad kar) straight upward and downward S.g.; po-bráṅ-gi tád-kar pyin (pho brang gi thad kar phyin) they came straight towards the castle; tád-ka-na (thad ka na) directly before Thgy.; dēi nub-tád-kyi (de'i nub thad kyi) that which is situated to the west of it Tar.; most frq. tád-du (thad du) c. genit. towards, in straight direction; over against; in presence of e.g. to assemble, to propound, to lay before one, to study under a professor Dzl.; exactly in the place of a thing Tar. 17, 1; śai tád-nas cod (sha'i thad nas chod) Tar. 159, 4 prob.: cut off only from the flesh; *té'-kya (the' kya), té'-kaṅ-la (the' kang la)* Ts. straight on; tad-draṅ-na (thad drang na) directly before Wdn.; *tad-nyá (thad nya)* W. over against, opposite, facing; tád-so-na (thad so na) = tád-ka-na (thad ka na) Mil. — 2. tád-kar (thad kar) each for himself Glr. — 3. entire, whole, untouched, safe (integer) C. and perh. Thgy.
frq. abbreviation for ཐམས་ཅད་ tams-cád (thams cad), whole, all.
Hind. थान, = yug, a piece of cloth.

1. resp. ysol-táb (gsol thab), fire-place, hearth, me-táb (me thab), id.; also for stove, lcags-táb (lcags thab) iron stove; tab śor (thab shor) 'the hearth is running over', i.e. the food placed on it runs over in boiling, a mis-hap the more serious, as the household god is offended by the evil smell caused thereby. — 2. v. sub caṅ (cang). Comp.: *táb-ka* (thab ka) W. fire-place, *táb-ka tsam yod* (thab ka tsam yod) how many fire-places, i.e. house-holds, are there? — tab-kúṅ (thab khung) opening or mouth of a stove, furnace, or fire-place; v. also Schl. 249. — tab-ynás (thab gnas) fire-place, furnace, oven Cs. — *tab-tsáṅ* (thab tshang) W. kitchen. — tab-pyís (thab phyis), W. *tab-pís* (thab phis) clout, dish-clout, wiper. — tab-rżób (thab rzhob) burnt smell. — *tab-lás có-kan* (thab las co mkhan) W. cook. — tab-yyóg (thab g.yog) kitchen-boy, scullion Pth. — tab-śiṅ (thab shing) fire-wood, fuel. — tab-lhá (thab lha) deity of the hearth.

(cog. to stabs (stabs)), opportunity, chance, possibility, *tóṅ-or d´ul-táb ma j´uṅ* (mthong ngor 'grul thabs ma byung) W. I had no opportunity of seeing or going; *tab śig nyi-ráṅ-ne mi j´uṅ-na* (thabs shig nyid rang nas mi byung na) W. if you offer no chance, if on your part it is not made possible; tabs mi tub (thabs mi thub) Dzl. and col. I am not able, I cannot; ydan-dráṅs-pai tabs med (gdan drangs pa'i thabs med) I then shall lose the opportunity of meeting (the princess) Glr.; b´ros-pai tabs med ('bros pa'i thabs med) there is not any chance of escape Glr.; lám-la yzól-tabs med (lam la gzol thabs med) there is no occasion for stopping or tarrying on the road Mil.; way, manner, mode, klog-tabs (klog thabs) way of reading, e.g. Sanskrit; rkún-tabs-su (rkun thabs su) in a thievish manner, by theft Stg.; rgyál-poi tabs ytóṅ-ba (rgyal po'i thabs gtong ba) to give up the way (of life) of a king, to resign the crown Dzl., tabs ycig-tu (thabs gcig tu) together, in company, jointly, e.g. to sit down with one another, to go together to a place, frq.; means, measures, tabs byéd-pa (thabs byed pa), W. *c´o-ce, kyóṅ-ce* (bcos ce, khyong ce) to use means, to take measures; blo tabs tsól-ba (blo thabs 'tshol ba) to contrive means Ma.; tabs stón-pa (thabs ston pa) to show means or ways, to give directions, to instruct Glr.; tsó-tabs ('tsho thabs) livelihood, subsistence; tabs zad (thabs zad) there is nothing else to be done Glr.; źi-bai tábs-kyis (zhi ba'i thabs kyis) in a fair way, amicably, not by constraint or compulsion Glr.; tábs-kyis (thabs kyis) by various means, by artifice, cunningly, craftily: tábs(-la)-mkás-pa (thabs (-la) mkhas pa), tábs-śes-pa (thabs shes pa), W. also *táb-can* (thabs can), skilful, dexterous, clever, full of devices; da bód-du gró-tabs gyis śig (da bod du 'gro thabs gyis shig) now take steps, make preparations, for a journey to Tibet Glr.; de ysón-poi tabs yód-dam (de gson po'i thabs yod dam) is there a means of recalling those men to life? tabs-c´ág (thabs chags) Mil., *tab-śág* (thab shag) or *teb-śág* (theb shag) vulgo, a shift, make-shift, surrogate; tabs (daṅ) śes (-rab) (thabs (dang) shes (-rab)) the mystical union of art and science, or (Sch. less correctly) of matter and spirit, cf. Was. (144).
a seal, sign Cs., v. dám-ka (dam ka).
